description:
Goal: make compiler2 native codegen compiler2-owned end to end, so the compiler2 route no longer depends on old-world semantic codegen or a second type owner before JIT/AOT. Signal: compiler2 JIT/AOT/runtime entry points consume a compiler2-owned native backend path whose semantic inputs are `NativeProgram(root)` plus the one world-owned compiler2 `Types`; any copied `SpecPlan`/`SpecRegistry`-shaped baggage is quarantined inside compiler2 during the move and then deleted from that path.

Grounding from the current code:
- compiler2 already closes its backend handoff as `NativeProgram` in `src/compiler2/artifact.rs`, with bodies, ABI lanes, value types, callable-entry inventory, closure-call targets, extern marshals, and effects.
- compiler2 still calls `ir_codegen::compile_with_backend_native_program` from `src/compiler2/compiler.rs`, seeding it with a fresh `crate::types::new()` and therefore a second type owner.
- the shared native-codegen path still rebuilds planner-shaped scaffolding for compiler2 in `src/ir_codegen/driver.rs`: synthetic `SpecKey`, fake `SpecPlan`, empty `SpecRegistry`, and `return_tys` widened to `any`.
- compiler2 native lowering still translates compiler2 facts back into old-world payloads in `src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs`: legacy extern contracts, legacy dispatch/guard structures, and `legacy_type_test_ty`.
- some backend/runtime mechanics are genuinely universal, but the code that reasons about types, dispatch, callable selection, extern contracts, helper ownership, and body identity is only shared on paper; in practice it is pinned to old-world inputs.

Strategy:
- publish the exact ownership cut first: what is universal substrate, what is semantic codegen, and which old-world shapes are illegal as the long-term compiler2-native contract.
- take one half-step back and fork the semantic native-codegen path into compiler2 largely intact, even if that temporarily carries `SpecPlan`/`SpecRegistry`-shaped baggage as compiler2-local implementation detail.
- switch compiler2 front doors onto that in-house backend immediately after parity is proven, so further cleanup happens on one side only.
- then simplify aggressively inside compiler2: delete copied planner-shaped baggage, stop reprojecting compiler2 facts into old `crate::types::Ty` payloads, and make the native handoff truthful by construction.
- close with docs/telemetry and restate the remaining migration problem, if any.

What should go away:
- compiler2 calls to `ir_codegen::compile_with_backend_native_program` and the fresh `crate::types::new()` in `src/compiler2/compiler.rs`.
- compiler2-native dependence on shared semantic codegen modules after `NativeProgram(root)`.
- copied planner-shaped baggage (`SpecPlan`, `SpecRegistry`, synthetic `SpecKey`, widened `return_tys`) once the in-house path is switched over and cleanup lands.
- compiler2-native re-projection of extern contracts, dispatch facts, or type tests into old `crate::types::Ty` / legacy dispatch payloads.

Checks:
- compiler2 quicksort, `Enum.reduce`, spawn/receive, variadic extern, and tail-recursion fixtures still run through JIT/AOT with identical behavior through the in-house backend.
- compiler2 telemetry still proves there is no fallback into planner/type-infer work, and backend telemetry makes the compiler2-native seam observable.
- docs describe the ownership cut and post-fork cleanup precisely enough that returning to `fz-rh2.9` is either tractable or obviously unnecessary.
title: Compiler2 native backends: bring native codegen in-house and delete old-world backend dead weight
